news.title: Hyderabad office leasing rises 29% to 7.5 million sq ft in H1 2026: Report
news.para: Hyderabad recorded office transactions of 7.5 million sq ft in H1 2026, up 29% year-on-year, while completions rose 111% to three million sq ft. GCCs accounted for 45% of total office leasing during the period.

news.title: Hyderabad housing sales rise 1% to 19,249 units in H1 2026: Report
news.para: Hyderabad recorded housing sales of 19,249 units in H1 2026, up one per cent year-on-year, while launches declined two per cent to 20,466 units. Unsold inventory rose three per cent to 56,095 units.

news.title: Bengaluru office leasing falls 23% to 14.1 million sq ft in H1 2026: Report
news.para: Bengaluru recorded office transactions of 14.1 million sq ft in H1 2026, down 23% year-on-year due to a high base in H1 2025. Office completions surged 407% to 10.4 million sq ft during the period.

news.title: Bengaluru housing sales rise 5% to 27,968 units in H1 2026: Report
news.para: Bengaluru recorded housing sales of 27,968 units in H1 2026, up five per cent year-on-year, while launches rose four per cent to 34,749 units. Unsold inventory increased 22 per cent to 74,299 units.

news.title: Property deals worth a record ₹17,543 crore registered in Nashik city in FY26
news.para: Nashik's property market achieved a record turnover of Rs 17,543 crore during the 2025-26 financial year. This significant growth reflects sustained real estate activity and evolving housing preferences post-pandemic. Regulatory reforms and the availability of ready homes also boosted property transactions across the city. Developers are seeing steady demand for apartments, driving continued registrations. High-rise developments are reshaping Nashik's skyline as urban growth accelerates.

news.title: Real estate firms ramp up legal hiring as deals grow more complex
news.para: In an evolving landscape, Indian real estate developers are strategically bolstering their legal departments. This growth represents a pivotal shift towards a more structured approach to deal-making. Leading developers are on the lookout for legal leaders and experts to adeptly handle various transactions, while law firms actively seek to recruit professionals from competitors. Moreover, effective legal planning has become integral to the overall business strategy for developers and their investors.

news.title: Mumbai: Politician-developer on the run after court denies bail in ₹7 crore fraud case
news.para: Shiv Sena politician Shankar Kumar Jha is now on the run from authorities.  His anticipatory bail plea was rejected by the Thane Sessions Court.  He faces allegations of cheating homebuyers of Rs 7.24 crore.  The investigation has been transferred to the Economic Offences Wing.  Jha’s current whereabouts remain unknown to the police.

news.title: Delhi CM seeks ₹100 crore Central fund for accelerating ownership scheme in unauthorised colonies
news.para: Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta requested Rs 100 crore from the Central government.  This funding is for accelerating the PM-UDAY scheme's implementation across Delhi.  The scheme aims to provide property rights to residents of 1,511 unauthorized colonies.  Proposed funds will establish district-level cells and a modern land survey system.  Public awareness campaigns and property verification exercises are also planned.

news.title: Mumbai office leasing rises 33% to 7.3 million sq ft in H1 2026: Report
news.para: Mumbai recorded gross office leasing of 7.3 million sq ft in H1 2026, up 33% year-on-year, while completions declined 30% to 1.6 million sq ft. Vacancy fell by 205 basis points to 15.6%.

news.title: Mumbai housing sales rise 1% to 47,355 units in H1 2026: Report
news.para: Mumbai recorded housing sales of 47,355 units in H1 2026, up one per cent year-on-year, while launches rose eight per cent to 49,161 units. Unsold inventory declined four per cent to 1,57,410 units.

news.title: NCR office leasing dips 1% to 7.2 million sq ft in H1 2026: Report
news.para: Office leasing in NCR stood at 7.2 million sq ft in H1 2026, down one per cent year-on-year, while completions declined two per cent to four million sq ft. Average transacted rents rose 13% to ₹106 per sq ft per month.

news.title: NCR housing sales fall 7% to 24,862 units in H1 2026: Report
news.para: NCR recorded residential sales of 24,862 units in H1 2026, down 7% year-on-year, while launches declined 5% to 23,877 units. Gurugram accounted for 42% of sales and 50% of new launches during the period.

news.title: Relief for Bhimrao Bada residents as Telangana HC orders 2BHK houses, ends 17-year dispute
news.para: The Telangana high court has ordered the state government to provide 2BHK houses. This decision brings closure to a seventeen-year legal battle for displaced families. Residents were evicted after their slum status was withdrawn in two thousand eight. The court directed allotments in Kollur within two months of receiving the order. Legal heirs of deceased petitioners will also be considered for these new homes.

news.title: Architects association urge Ranchi civic body to end building plan approval deadlock
news.para: Licensed technical professionals in Jharkhand are concerned about building map approval delays.  Many property owners face rejections despite obtaining holding numbers.  The association argues these rejections contradict established building bye-laws.  They urge the municipal commissioner to expedite map approvals for valid holding holders.  A meeting is planned to discuss these ongoing issues in detail.

news.title: Renting a house in Chandigarh likely to soon go fully digital
news.para: Chandigarh administration has proposed new digital tenancy rules for registration and dispute resolution.  These rules aim to create a fully online framework for all rental agreements.  Landlords and tenants must submit tenancy details via a dedicated online portal.  The proposed system will establish a digital repository for all tenancy records.  These rules will become operational after public objections and potential court decisions.

news.title: Collector’s office not executing RERA’s refund orders: Bombay HC
news.para: The Bombay High Court observed a sorry state of affairs with the collector’s office.  MahaRERA decrees are not being executed scrupulously by the collectors’ office.  The court issued notices to the collector and tehsildar for failing to abide by its order.  A court receiver was appointed over a flat to secure buyer interests.  The court seeks to prevent builders from defeating RERA provisions for buyer refunds.

news.title: Produce approved, advertised drawings of Panchkula flat: Haryana REAT to DLF
news.para: The Haryana Real Estate Appellate Tribunal has ordered a realty firm and the DTCP to produce specific project drawings. This direction follows a homebuyer's challenge regarding the apartment's carpet area and pricing calculations. The tribunal also requested the occupation certificate based on the approved drawings for verification. The realty firm has disputed the homebuyer's allegations concerning the sale price calculations.

news.title: Real estate developers are modernizing channel loyalty for brokers and agents
news.para: Real estate developers must enhance broker loyalty beyond commissions.  Modern programs focus on engagement, recognition, and performance visibility.  Brokers now expect flexible rewards and transparent communication from developers.  Benepik offers a platform to automate and manage these crucial loyalty initiatives.  Investing in partner experience drives sustainable growth and builds trusted advocates.

news.title: The Executive Centre leases 1.15 lakh sq ft office space in Delhi’s Aerocity
news.para: The Executive Centre India has leased 1.15 lakh sq ft office space at Worldmark 6, Aerocity, Delhi, from Alborz Developers. The company will pay about ₹309 crore rent over the nine-year lease tenure.

news.title: About 199 buildings flagged as unsafe in Lucknow; Owners told to repair or vacate
news.para: Lucknow Municipal Corporation has identified 199 unsafe buildings requiring immediate repair or evacuation. These structures pose significant risks to residents and nearby properties during heavy rainfall. The highest number of dangerous buildings are located in Zone 1, which includes Hazratganj and Aminabad areas. Many of these older buildings have weakened due to age and poor maintenance over time.
